[[75th Berlin International Film Festival|75th]] }} The '''74th annual Berlin International Film Festival''', usually called the '''Berlinale''', took place between 15 and 25 February 2024 in [[Berlin]], Germany.{{Cite web |title=Berlinale Special 2024: First Film Titles |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/2024/news-press-releases/248004.html |access-date=2023-12-20 |website=www.berlinale.de |language=en}} Kenyan-Mexican actress [[Lupita Nyong'o]] was named the Jury President for the main competition.{{Cite web |title=Academy Award Winner Lupita Nyong'o to Be Jury President of the Berlinale 2024 |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/2024/news-press-releases/247299.html |date=2023-12-11 |access-date=2023-12-12 |website=www.berlinale.de |language=en}}{{cite news |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/berlinale-2024-sidebar-lineup-panorama-generation-forum-schwartzman-huppert-ronan-1235790569/ |title=Middle East, Gender Politics in Focus in Berlin Sidebars |first=Scott |last=Roxborough |date=17 January 2024 |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] }} This year's Berlinale was [[Carlo Chatrian]] and Mariette Rissenbeek's final edition in charge, following their dismissal in 2023.{{Cite news |last=Roxborough |first=Scott |date=2023-09-02 |title=Berlin Film Festival Artistic Director Carlo Chatrian to Step Down After 2024 Edition |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/berlin-film-festival-director-carlo-chatrain-step-down-2024-1235580707/ |access-date=2024-02-26 |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] | language=en-US }} The festival opened with Tim Mielants' ''[[Small Things Like These (film)|Small Things Like These]].''{{Cite web |title=''Small Things Like These'' Starring Cillian Murphy Set to Open Berlinale |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/2024/news-press-releases/249794.html |date=2024-01-18 |access-date=2024-01-18 |website=www.berlinale.de |language=en}}{{cite news |title=Berlin Film Festival: Christian Petzold, Ann Hui & Albert Serra Among Names Joining Lupita Nyong'o's International Jury |date=1 February 2024 |url=https://deadline.com/2024/02/berlinale-christian-petzold-ann-hui-albert-serra-lupita-nyongo-international-jury-1235810833/ |first=Zac |last=Ntim |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] }} ''[[Dahomey (film)|Dahomey]]'', directed by French-Senegalese filmmaker [[Mati Diop]], won the [[Golden Bear]], making it the second year in a row that a documentary won the festival's top prize, following ''[[On the Adamant]]''{{'}}s win in 2023. The [[Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize]] was awarded to ''[[A Traveler's Needs]] by'' [[Hong Sang-soo]], and the [[Silver Bear for Best Leading Performance]] was awarded to [[Sebastian Stan]] for ''[[A Different Man]]''.{{Cite web |title=The Prizes of the International Jury |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/festival/awards-and-juries/international-jury.html |access-date=2024-02-24 |website=www.berlinale.de |language=en}}{{Cite news |last=Lodge |first=Guy |date=2024-02-24 |title=Mati Diop Doc ''Dahomey'' Wins Golden Bear at Berlin; Sebastian Stan and Emily Watson Take Acting Awards |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/festivals/berlin-film-festival-awards-updating-live-1235921363/ |access-date=2024-02-24 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] | language=en-US}} American filmmaker [[Martin Scorsese]] was awarded with the [[Honorary Golden Bear]].{{Cite web |title=The Honorary Golden Bear |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/festival/awards-and-juries/honorary-golden-bear.html |access-date=2024-02-08 |website=www.berlinale.de |language=en}} == Juries == [[File:MKr347546 Lupita Nyong'o (Jury, Berlinale 2024) crop.jpg|thumb|180x180px|[[Lupita Nyong'o]], Jury President for the Main Competition]] [[File:International Jury, Berlinale 2024.jpg|thumb|236x236px|Main Competition ''[[Crossing (2024 film)|Crossing]]'' by [[Levan Akin]] (Panorama) * '''Special Teddy Award''': Lothar Lambert == Controversies == === Iranian censorship === Shortly before the Iranian film ''[[My Favourite Cake]]'' was selected for the Main Competition, filmmakers [[Behtash Sanaeeha]] and [[Maryam Moqadam]] were banned from leaving [[Iran]] to attend the Festival, had their passports confiscated, and will face a court trial in relation to their work as artists and filmmakers.{{Cite web |title=Berlinale Issues Call for Freedom of Movement, Freedom of Expression for Competition Directors Maryam Moghaddam & Behtash Sanaeeha |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/2024/news-press-releases/251138.html |access-date=2024-02-04 |website=www.berlinale.de |language=en}}{{cite news |date=February 2024 |title=Berlinale Makes Appeal For Iranian 2024 Golden Bear Contenders Maryam Moghaddam & Behtash Sanaeeha As They Barred From Travel |url=https://deadline.com/2024/02/berlinale-appeal-iranian-fllmmakers-maryam-moghaddam-behtash-sanaeeha-1235810748/ |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]]}} The Iranian government decision was met, once again, with international protests, following [[Golden Bear]] winners [[Jafar Panahi]] and [[Mohammad Rasoulof]] arrests in 2022/2023, and numerous others [[Censorship in Iran|censorship]] attempts in the last years.{{Cite news |last=Goodfellow |first=Melanie |date=2024-02-01 |title=Berlinale Makes Appeal For Iranian 2024 Golden Bear Contenders Maryam Moghaddam & Behtash Sanaeeha As They Barred From Travel |url=https://deadline.com/2024/02/berlinale-appeal-iranian-fllmmakers-maryam-moghaddam-behtash-sanaeeha-1235810748/ |access-date=2024-02-06 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] | language=en-US}}{{cite news |date=6 February 2024 |title=Leading German Filmmaker Says Berlinale Far-Right Invitation Is "Highly Problematic" |url=https://deadline.com/2024/02/berlinale-berlin-afd-far-right-invitation-highly-problematic-dennis-gansel-1235815926/ |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]]}} === Rescinsion of AfD invitiation === Amid controversy, Berlinale's directors Mariette Rissenbeek and [[Carlo Chatrian]] rescinded its decision to invite representatives of the far-right party [[Alternative for Germany]] ({{langx|de|Alternative für Deutschland}}, {{lang|de|AfD}}) to attend the festival's Opening Ceremony Gala. The decision followed a number of controversies around the party's statements in [[opposition to immigration]].{{Cite web |title=Berlinale Opening: Disinvitation of AfD Politicians |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/2024/news-press-releases/252233.html |access-date=2024-02-10 |website=www.berlinale.de |language=en}}{{cite news |date=8 February 2024 |title=Berlin Film Festival Disinvites Far-Right Politicians From Opening Ceremony After Backlash |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/global/berlinale-disinvites-far-right-officials-opening-ceremony-1235902214/ |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]}}{{Cite news |last=Keslassy |first=Elsa |date=2024-02-08 |title=Berlin Film Festival Disinvites Far-Right Politicians From Opening Ceremony After Backlash |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/global/berlinale-disinvites-far-right-officials-opening-ceremony-1235902214/ |access-date=2024-02-09 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] | language=en-US}} An open letter was signed by over 200 German cultural industry professionals expressing outrage with the invitations.{{Cite news |last=Dalton |first=Ben |date=2024-02-08 |title=Berlinale disinvites far-right AfD politicians from opening ceremony |url=https://www.screendaily.com/news/berlinale-disinvites-far-right-afd-politicians-from-opening-ceremony/5190340.article |access-date=2024-02-09 |work=[[Screen International]] | language=en}}{{cite news |date=15 February 2024 |title=Lupita Nyong'o on Berlinale Disinviting Far-Right Politicians From Opening Ceremony: 'I'm Glad I Don't Have to Be in That Position' |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/festivals/lupita-nyongo-berlinale-far-right-politicians-afd-1235907701/ |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]}} === Gaza war protests === [[File:Lights Camera Genocide Berlinale 2024.jpg|thumb|Protesters unfurled a "Lights Camera Genocide" banner at the 2024 European Film Market, arguing that the Berlinale and Germany are complicit in another genocide.]] In the introductory press release for the 2024 festival, Berlinale opted not to mention freedom of speech as one of their core values, despite having done so in the previous year's statement.{{Cite web |title=Berlinale Statement |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/2024/news-press-releases/249881.html |access-date=2024-02-27 |website=www.berlinale.de |language=en}}{{Cite web |title=Berlinale 2023: Solidarity with Ukraine and Iran |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/2023/news-press-releases/229058.html |access-date=2024-02-27 |website=www.berlinale.de |language=en}}{{verify source|date=February 2024}} During the festival, hundreds of past and present Berlinale participants signed open letters criticizing Berlinale's complicity in Germany's censorship of pro-Palestine voices amidst the backdrop of the [[Gaza war]], including over 280 [[Berlinale Talents]] alumni,{{Cite web |title=A statement from Berlinale Talents alumni |url=https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdCvUMI3PeAp_Kkjeyg1Sz8iDc63kXe_GZIrpZ6rDADbXJd5g/viewform?vc=0&c=0&w=1&flr=0&usp=embed_facebook |access-date=2024-02-27 |website=Google Docs |language=en}} over 190 filmmakers with films in the 2024 festival{{Cite web |title=A letter from filmmakers participating in the 74th Berlinale |url=https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdDWR14LYGuevCblXzWAMCNhmYnS4JKWFVQX4JFa_l5L4sYwg/viewform?edit_requested=true&usp=embed_facebook |access-date=2024-02-27 |website=Google Docs |language=en}} and over 60 Berlinale contractors.{{Cite web |title=A statement from Berlinale workers – adding our voices |url=https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSc1boJlSYx8WaMbErnc3hGgbrotLTULmaEapYpcu5bVMsK5Kw/viewform?usp=embed_facebook |access-date=2024-02-27 |website=Google Docs |language=en}}{{verify source|date=February 2024}} In further protest, [[John Greyson]], Suneil Sanzgiri and [[Ayo Tsalithaba]] all withdrew their films from the festival, while Maryam Tafakory, Advik Beni, and [[Monica Sorelle]] dropped out of the Berlinale Talents programme, and Emilia Beatriz withdrew from the [[European Film Market]].{{Cite web |last=Girish |first=Devika |date=2024-03-11 |title=Worlds Apart: Berlinale 2024 |url=https://www.filmcomment.com/blog/no-other-land-palestine-strike-germany-berlinale-2024/ |access-date=2024-04-03 |website=Film Comment |language=en}} Unlike its response to the public's outrage at the AfD invitation, Berlinale directors remained silent to the demands of their filmmakers, alumni, and contractors in support of Palestine. Throughout the festival, artists continued to use their platforms to make statements in solidarity with [[Palestine]]. On February 16, curators of the Berlinale's Forum Expanded program joined artist's expressions of solidarity, stating, "We, too, want to add our voice and share our concern by expressing that the Forum Expanded curators support the urgent call for an immediate ceasefire in Gaza."{{Cite web |last=Pontone |first=Maya |date=2024-02-16 |title=Berlinale Film Festival Curators Join Call for Gaza Ceasefire |url=http://hyperallergic.com/872536/berlinale-film-festival-curators-join-call-for-gaza-ceasefire/ |access-date=2024-02-27 |website=Hyperallergic |language=en-US}} On February 18, [[Palestinian nationalism|pro-Palestine]] activists laid down on the front steps of the [[Martin-Gropius-Bau|Gropius Bau]] drenched in fake blood with a sign reading, “Welcome to the Red Carpet,” while inside the building others unfurled pro-Palestinian banners from the upper floor.{{Cite web |last=Meza |first=Ed |date=2024-02-24 |title=Hillary Clinton, Film Market Hit by Protests in Berlin as Pro-Palestinian Group Calls for Boycott of German Cultural Institutions |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/global/pro-palestine-protests-berlinale-hillary-clinton-efm-1235920436/ |access-date=2024-02-27 |website=Variety |language=en-US}} During the Closing Night Ceremony at the Berlinale Palast, on February 25, there were numerous pro-Palestine statements and protests during the red carpet and acceptance speeches, including from [[Golden Bear]] winner [[Mati Diop]], and Palestinian and Israeli filmmakers [[Basel Adra]], [[Hamdan Ballal]], [[Yuval Abraham]], and Rachel Szor, the four of whom directed ''[[No Other Land]]''. The [[Teddy Award]] jury posted a statement in solidarity with [[Gaza Strip|Gaza]] which was met with audience applause as well as loud booing.{{Cite web |title=Instagram |url=https://www.instagram.com/p/C3xftn5t5Q9/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA== |access-date=2024-02-27 |website=www.instagram.com}}{{verify source|date=February 2024}} An [[Instagram]] account linked to the Panorama section published an allegedly official statement from the festival organizers, stating "we acknowledge that our silence makes us complicit in Israel's ongoing [[Gaza genocide|genocide in Gaza]] and ethnic cleansing of Palestine"{{Cite web |last=Welk |first=Brian |date=2024-02-26 |title=Berlinale Files Criminal Charges Against Hackers Who Posted Gaza Ceasefire Message |url=https://www.indiewire.com/news/festivals/berlinale-files-criminal-charges-against-hackers-gaza-ceasefire-message-1234957531/ |access-date=2024-02-27 |website=IndieWire |language=en-US}} adding: "From our unresolved [[Nazi Germany|Nazi]] past to our genocidal present – we have always been on the wrong side of history."{{Cite web |last=Ntim |first=Zac |date=2024-02-26 |title=Berlin Mayor Criticizes "Anti-Semitic" Berlinale Closing Ceremony As Hackers Post Ceasefire Message To Fest's Social |url=https://deadline.com/2024/02/berlin-film-festival-hackers-gaza-ceasefire-kai-wegner-anti-semitism-1235838268/ |access-date=2024-02-27 |website=Deadline |language=en-US}} Minutes later, the Berlinale's main Instagram account stated that the Panorama account was hacked and the posts "do not represent the Berlinale's position", and announced plans to “file criminal charges against unknown persons”. During his acceptance speech after winning the Best Documentary award, ''No Other Land'' co-director Abraham stated, referring to his Palestinian co-director Adra: "I am under civilian law; Basel [Adra] is under military law. We live 30 minutes from one another but I have voting rights. Basel does not have voting rights. I am free to move where I want in this land. Basel, like millions of Palestinians, is locked in the [[Israeli occupation of the West Bank|occupied West Bank]]. This situation of [[Israeli apartheid|apartheid]] between us, this inequality, has to end".{{Cite web |last=Bergeson |first=Samantha |date=2024-02-26 |title='No Other Land' Director Received 'Death Threats' After Calling for Gaza Ceasefire at Berlinale |url=https://www.indiewire.com/news/festivals/no-other-land-director-death-threats-after-berlinale-speech-1234957370/ |access-date=2024-02-27 |website=IndieWire |language=en-US}} [[Governing Mayor of Berlin|Berlin Mayor]], [[Kai Wegner]], and numerous other German politicians expressed outrage, calling the closing ceremony statements "anti-Semitic". Germany's minister of state for culture [[Claudia Roth]] was criticized for clapping during Adra and Abraham's speech, and she later claimed that she was only clapping for Abraham, declaring that "The statements at the Bears ceremony were shockingly one-sided and characterized by a profound hatred of Israel".{{cite web |date=26 February 2024 |title=Wegner und Roth kündigen Aufarbeitung von Israel-Kritik bei Berlinale an |url=https://www.rbb24.de/kultur/berlinale/beitraege/2024/berlinale-israel-kritik-reaktionen-claudia-roth-aufarbeitung-weg.html |access-date=27 February 2024 |website=www.rbb24.de}}{{Cite news |last=Oltermann |first=Philip |date=2024-02-27 |title=German minister says she clapped Israeli film-maker, not his Palestinian colleague, at Berlinale |url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2024/feb/27/german-minister-says-she-was-only-applauding-israeli-filmmaker-at-berlinale |access-date=2024-02-27 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}} While the Festival is mainly funded by the [[Cabinet of Germany|German government]],{{Cite web |last=Sharma |first=Gouri |title='Complete censorship': Germany's Palestinian diaspora fights crackdown |url=https://www.aljazeera.com/features/2023/10/26/complete-censorship-germanys-palestinian-diaspora-fights-crackdown |access-date=2024-02-27 |website=Al Jazeera |language=en}} the organizers stated that the "filmmakers' statements were independent and should be accepted as long as they respect the [[legality of Holocaust denial#Germany|legal framework]]".{{Cite news |date=2024-02-26 |title=Berlinale film festival marred by 'antisemitic' protests |url=https://www.euronews.com/culture/2024/02/26/berlinale-film-festival-marred-by-antisemitic-protests |access-date=2024-02-26 |work=[[euronews]] | language=en}}{{Cite news |last=Vicente |first=Álex |date=2024-02-20 |title=The Berlinale on the war front: How the Israel–Palestine conflict undermined the film festival |url=https://english.elpais.com/culture/2024-02-20/the-berlinale-on-the-war-front-how-the-israel-palestine-conflict-undermined-the-film-festival.html |access-date=2024-02-26 |work=[[El País]] | language=en-us}}{{cite news |last=Frater |first=Patrick |date=2024-02-26 |title=Berlin Film Festival Files Criminal Charges After Anti-Semitic Hacking, Criticizes Award-Winner Statements |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/news/berlin-festival-anti-semitic-hacking-award-winner-statements-1235923611/ |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]}} Following the ceremony, Abraham said that a right-wing mob in Israel had threatened his family, stating, "The appalling misuse of this word by Germans... to silence Israelis like me who support a ceasefire... empties the word antisemitism of meaning and thus endangers Jews all over the world".{{cite web |last1=Vlessing |first1=Etan |date=27 February 2024 |title=Israeli Filmmaker Slams German Politicians for Branding Berlin Award-Winning Speech "Antisemitic" |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/israeli-filmmaker-berlin-awards-speech-antisemitic-threats-1235837583/ |access-date=28 February 2024 |website=The Hollywood Reporter}} == References == == External links == {{Commons}} * {{Official website|https://www.berlinale.de/en/home.html}} {{Berlin International Film Festival}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Berlin
